text.title: Brainstorming Techniques Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Facilitator (or a senior in the room) interrupts generation with 'that won't work' — wild ideas stop, group settles on safe ones.
antipattern.root-cause: Defer-judgment rule was not enforced by the facilitator script.
antipattern.fix: Add an explicit 'evaluation-during-gen = facilitator pause' line to the session plan; commit to it visibly at session start.

antipattern.symptom: One participant produces 60% of the spoken ideas in a Classic session; introverts disengage by minute 10.
antipattern.fix: Switch to Round Robin (enforced turns) or Brainwriting (written) when ≥1 dominant speaker is in the group.

antipattern.symptom: Session emits a 70-line raw list; PM cannot act on it; ideas die in a doc.
antipattern.fix: Mandate the post-generation 4-min cluster + 2-min score block; output without scored_shortlist is rejected.

antipattern.symptom: First idea in Classic sets a frame; subsequent ideas are variations of it; novelty dies.
antipattern.fix: Use Brainwriting 6-3-5 to silence anchoring; or use Round Robin with rotated first-speaker.
